evals.prompt: Should I create a new type for 'DeFi protocol', or use an existing type?
evals.expected_output: Advice grounded in ONTOLOGY.md + the live graph: prefer a broad existing type unless a narrow one adds real properties; suggests the existing type or justifies a new one. Read-only — drafts advice, never writes.
assertions: Checks the live graph for an existing comparable type before recommending a new one
Applies broad-over-narrow (only create a narrow type if it adds >= 2 meaningful properties)
Gives advice only — does not create or modify anything on the graph

evals.prompt: For a Person entity, should 'author' be a relation or a property?
evals.expected_output: Recommends a relation, because the value is a real-world thing (another entity), per the relations-over-free-text principle.
assertions: Recommends modeling 'author' as a relation, not a text property
Justifies it with the relations-over-free-text rule (the value is a real-world entity)
